behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the watcher recursion boundary (watch root -> nested path event -> pipeline trigger), not just local parsing logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: recursive file-watch behavior depends on real OS filesystem events and watcher backend semantics; in-process fakes can miss real missed-event bugs. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely filesystem/watcher behavior with no AI/ML decision ('file placed inside a subdirectory... is processed'), so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a file in a nested subdirectory, and assert one transformed output is produced without manual rescans.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior targets dedup decision logic for same path + time window, which can be exercised at a single object/function boundary with a controllable clock. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess boundary in the behavior text ('second processing request'). Rejected Integration: no concrete text requiring cross-module handoff; rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because no real infra or AI dependency is described.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component directly with the same normalized path twice under a fake clock and configurable window; assert first request accepted, second rejected, and acceptance resumes after window expiry.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure mode is explicitly OS event burst behavior ('operating system emits multiple filesystem events'). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher callbacks can hide backend-specific duplicate event patterns and timing. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call or multi-agent sequence in this behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real filesystem watching, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write+rename), and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is about live OS directory lifecycle events (delete/recreate) and watcher reattachment while process stays up. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes do not faithfully reproduce watch-handle invalidation and re-subscription edge cases. Rejected higher levels: text is filesystem daemon resilience only, with no model decision steps, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, delete the directory, recreate it at the same path, then create a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering correctness depends on interaction of concurrent arrival events, filesystem timestamp reads, and queueing. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: synthetic calls can miss real race/timestamp-granularity issues that appear only with real filesystem/event timing. Rejected higher levels: behavior text concerns file arrival ordering only; no AI/ML output dependence or multi-agent chain, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a real watched directory, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled creation times, then assert processing/output order matches the files' creation timestamps.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at the boundary between real write-in-progress OS/file semantics and daemon readiness checks. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked file handles or immediate writes can hide partial-write timing bugs. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is filesystem completeness gating, not AI-dependent decision quality or multi-agent workflow, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: With the daemon running, stream-write a large file in chunks to the watched directory while keeping it open; assert no processing starts until writer closes/completes, then exactly one processing run occurs.
